description: Analyze household vehicle ownership patterns and identify transit-dependent zones in San Francisco using the UrbanSim dataset. Working within the Jupyter notebook at /home/ga/urbansim_projects/notebooks/transit_dependency_analysis.ipynb, utilize the 'households', 'buildings', and 'parcels' tables from /home/ga/urbansim_projects/data/sanfran_public.h5 to associate households with their zone_id (linking households to buildings via building_id, and buildings to parcels via parcel_id). The analysis requires calculating zone-level metrics for total_households, mean_cars, pct_zero_car, and median_income, flagging zones as 'transit_dependent' where pct_zero_car is greater than 30. Export the resulting summary table to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/zone_vehicle_ownership.csv with the columns zone_id, total_households, mean_cars, pct_zero_car, median_income, and transit_dependent. Additionally, generate and save three visualizations to /home/ga/urbansim_projects/output/: vehicle_ownership_histogram.png, income_vs_cars_scatter.png, and transit_dependency_by_zone.png (showing the top 15 zones by pct_zero_car). The final notebook must be fully executed.
